title: Fix a unique-constraint collision between async tests
prompt: A module has 5 async tests that each do `{:ok, user} = Accounts.create_user(%{email: "test@example.com"})`. Under concurrent execution, 2-3 tests sometimes fail with `Ecto.Changeset.unique_violation`. Rewrite `test/my_app/accounts_test.exs`: (1) for each test, use a UUID-based email via `"user_#{Ecto.UUID.generate()}@example.com"`, (2) preserve `async: true`, (3) explain in a comment that hard-coded unique fields do NOT work in async because the sandbox rollback happens at test end, but the uniqueness check happens DURING the test.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the uniqueness-check timing detail, not just the fix pattern.
